lede: A look at December 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ander Barrenetxea (footballer) and Rick Porcello, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Gelsenkirchen heist
events.desc: An estimated €30 million were stolen from a Sparkasse bank in Gelsenkirchen, Germany.

events.title: 2009 Iranian presidential election protests
events.desc: Iranian election protests: On the Day of Ashura in Tehran, Iran, government security forces fire upon demonstrators.

events.title: Mombasa
events.desc: Riots erupt in Mombasa, Kenya, after Mwai Kibaki is declared the winner of the presidential election, triggering a political, economic, and humanitarian crisis.

events.title: Scandinavian Airlines System Flight 751
events.desc: Scandinavian Airlines System Flight 751 crashes in Gottröra in the Norrtälje Municipality in Sweden, injuring 92.

events.title: Indonesian National Revolution
events.desc: Indonesian National Revolution: The Netherlands officially recognizes Indonesian independence. End of the Dutch East Indies.

events.title: 1939 Erzincan earthquake
events.desc: The 7.8 Mw Erzincan earthquake shakes eastern Turkey with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme). At least 32,700 people were killed.

events.title: Winter War
events.desc: Winter War: Finland holds off a Soviet attack in the Battle of Kelja.

events.title: Radio City Music Hall
events.desc: Radio City Music Hall, "Showplace of the Nation", opens in New York City.

events.title: Manifest destiny
events.desc: Having coined the phrase "manifest destiny" the previous July, journalist John L. O'Sullivan argued in his newspaper New York Morning News that the United States had the right to claim the entire Oregon Country.

events.title: Hagia Sophia
events.desc: The second Hagia Sophia in Constantinople is consecrated.

deaths.text: Harry Carey Jr., Jr., American actor, producer, and screenwriter
Benazir Bhutto, Pakistani politician, Prime Minister of Pakistan
J. B. L. Reyes, Filipino lawyer and jurist
Robert Leke, 3rd Earl of Scarsdale, English earl, politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pope Maximus of Alexandria
